update_schedule_json.

commit.message:
fix: address security vulnerabilities in unsafe memory access and XML parsing (#82)

- Mitigate XXE attacks by disabling DTD processing and external entity
  resolution when loading XML schemas (SBESourceGenerator.cs)
- Break on failed TryRead in group consumption loop to prevent runaway
  reads from malicious NumInGroup values (MessageDefinition.cs)
- Validate blockLength against buffer.Length before Slice in TryParse
  backward compatibility path (MessageDefinition.cs)
- Use checked arithmetic in SumFieldLength to detect integer overflow
  from malicious schema field lengths (FileContentGeneratorExtensions.cs)
- Add SBE007 diagnostic warning when schema specifies non-native byte
  order, since the generator does not emit endianness conversion logic
- Update snapshot tests to reflect new bounds check in generated code

commit.message:
Upgrade rhythm tracking to production YOLO+KLT+Kalman architecture

- Replaced primitive blob detection with YOLO neural network
- Added Pyramid KLT optical flow tracking for subpixel accuracy
- Implemented Kalman filter for temporal smoothing
- Added hybrid fallback chain: YOLO → Blob detection → Kalman prediction
- Preserved all rhythm analysis logic unchanged
- Integrated ONNX Runtime for model inference
- Maintained 100% backward compatibility

Changes:
- Lines: 668 → 959 (+291 lines, +44%)
- Added 18 new tracking functions
- Added 50+ configuration constants
- Updated capture loop for hybrid tracking
- Updated render loop for real-time display with fallback

Expected improvements:
- 60-70% better tracking robustness

feat: add App.get_app_graph() and App._get_graph_token() (#355)

`App` had no way to access a Graph client for app-only operations — only
`ActivityContext` did.

## Changes

- **`App._get_graph_token(tenant_id=None)`** — new private method
wrapping `_token_manager.get_graph_token()`, mirroring the existing
`_get_bot_token` pattern.

- **`App.get_app_graph(tenant_id=None)`** — new public method returning
a `GraphServiceClient` for app-only Graph operations. Not cached
(clients own caching if needed). Accepts optional `tenant_id` for
multi-tenant support.

- **`utils/graph.py`** — new utility module with a public
`create_graph_client` helper (replaces the private `_get_graph_client`
inline function in `activity_context.py`). Both `ActivityContext` and
`App` now import it from `utils`.

- **`ActivityContext.app_graph`** — unchanged.

- **`ActivityProcessor`** — unchanged.

```python
# App-level Graph access, no ActivityContext needed
graph = app.get_app_graph()                         # default tenant
graph = app.get_app_graph(tenant_id="some-tenant")  # explicit tenant
me = await graph.me.get()
```

watchdog: fix fractional timestamp parsing in daemon_recently_active()

journalctl --output=short-unix produces timestamps like 1234567890.123456
on some systemd versions. The regex ^[0-9]+\$ rejects the decimal point,
causing daemon_recently_active() to always return false. The busy-streak
guard (rpc_busy_streak) therefore never fires and every RPC timeout goes
straight to fail_streak, triggering unnecessary restarts of a live node.

Fix: strip fractional seconds with \ before the regex check.

form on the left while always keeping scenarios and results visible on the right.
